---
title: Market Research — E42-S2 Positive Fixture
date: 2026-04-24
---

# Market Research: Structured Ideation Platform

## Executive Summary

This market research analyzes the structured ideation platform opportunity with a global geographic scope. The market spans product teams worldwide running ideation sessions.

## Market Definition

- Market: Collaborative structured ideation tooling for product teams.
- Geographic scope: global, with initial focus on North America and Europe.

## Competitive Analysis

Competitive positioning matrix:

| Competitor | Lightweight | Structured Output | Price |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Miro       | Medium      | Low               | $$    |
| FigJam     | Medium      | Low               | $     |
| Notion     | Low         | Medium            | $$    |

### Miro

Strengths: broad canvas flexibility, large template library, established enterprise adoption.
Weaknesses: output is unstructured freeform, expensive enterprise tiers, steep new-user learning curve.

### FigJam

Strengths: tight Figma integration for design teams, fast real-time collaboration, low price point.
Weaknesses: limited structured export, designer-centric, weak PRD handoff.

### Notion

Strengths: flexible document model, strong cross-team adoption, native database features.
Weaknesses: slow for real-time ideation, no opinionated ideation templates, scattered outputs.

## Customer Segments

Target segments defined with evidence from 12 customer interviews:

- Early-stage product teams (3-8 people) — evidence: 9/12 interviews cited unstructured ideation pain.
- Product consultants running client workshops — evidence: 4/12 cited reusable artifact need.

User behavior patterns identified:

- Teams default to whiteboards, then export screenshots that decay in Slack.
- Parking-lot ideas are rarely revisited; 8/12 teams reported losing ideas across sessions.

Underserved needs highlighted:

- Structured, machine-readable ideation output that feeds downstream PRD automation.
- Parking-lot revival triggers tied to product milestones.

## Market Sizing

TAM estimate: $8B globally.
TAM assumptions: 40M product professionals worldwide, $200 annual ACV per seat, 100% adoption ceiling.

SAM estimate: $1.6B.
SAM assumptions: addressable subset of English-speaking product teams under 50 employees, ~20% of TAM.

SOM estimate: $80M over 3 years.
SOM assumptions: 5% of SAM realistic given current go-to-market footprint, ramp over 36 months.

## Web Access

Web access was available during this session — live competitive research was incorporated.

## Key Findings

- Structured ideation output is the top cited pain.
- Parking-lot revival is a differentiator no incumbent addresses.
- PRD handoff automation is the highest-leverage follow-on feature.

## Strategic Recommendations

- Ship opinionated structured ideation MVP within one quarter.
- Pair parking-lot capture with revival triggers from day one.
- Prioritize PRD handoff integration for the following quarter.
